LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF STANLEY SHEN'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS UNDER CCP 128.7 IS DENIED. THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS AS REQUIRED BY LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOREOVER, IT APPEARS THAT THE MOTION IS PROCEDURALLY IMPROPER SINCE DEFENDANTS WERE NOT GIVEN THE REQUIRED 21 DAY SAFE HARBOR PERIOD TO WITHDRAW THEIR DEMURRER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, NOT REPORTED. = (302/HEK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ALICE TOY AND HA HUYNH'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERULED. WHILE THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS AS REQUIRED BY LOCAL RULE 2.6B WHICH MADE IT HARDER FOR THE COURT TO DECIDE THIS MATTER, THE DEMURRER APPEARS WITHOUT MERIT FOR TWO REASONS. FIRST, THE ORDER FILED JANUARY 6 OVERRULED DEFENDANTS' DEMURRERS TO THE FIRST FIVE CLAIMS AND SUCCESSIVE DEMURRERS ARE NOT PERMITTED. SECOND, MR. SHEN ALLEGES THAT HE WAS PERSONALLY DAMAGES AND HE APPEARS TO BE A PARTY TO THE LEASE DATED OCTOBER 25, 2010. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, NOT REPORTED. = (302/HEK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ALICE TOY AND HA HUYNH'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST THROUGH FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE FIRST FIVE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. WHETHER THE CONTRACTUAL TERM "FIXTURE" COVERS SOME OF ALL OF THE SEWER SYSTEM IS NOT AMENABLE TO RESOLUTION ON A DEMURRER. A CLAIM FOR BREACH OF THE WARRANTY OF HABITABILITY IS NOT AVAILABLE UNLESS THERE ARE INHABITABLE CONDITIONS, BUT THAT IS NOT THE CASE FOR A NON-RESIDENTIAL RESTAURANT. ORDER SIGNED. Notice Ofmotion And Motion For Sanctions Under Ccp 128.7; Memo Of P&A
Off Calendar Mar-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SUZAN YEE, TEL. NO. 371-8811 APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF STANLEY SHEN'S Motion For Sanctions Under Ccp 128.7 IS Denied. The moving party failed to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers as required by Local Rule 2.6B. Moreover, it appears that the motion is procedurally improper since defendants were not given the required 21 day safe harbor period to withdraw their demurrer. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Not reported. = (302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SUZAN YEE, TEL. NO. 371-8811 APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ALICE TOY AND HA HUYNH'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Overuled. While the court did not receive courtesy copies of the opposition papers as required by Local Rule 2.6B which made it harder for the court to decide this matter, the demurrer appears without merit for two reasons. First, the order filed January 6 overruled defendants' demurrers to the first five claims and successive demurrers are not permitted. Second, Mr. Shen alleges that he was personally damages and he appears to be a party to the lease dated October 25, 2010. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Not reported. = (302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-27-2012 Continued To Mar-29-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ALICE TOY AND HA HUYNH'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Stanley Shen (994.5853) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: overruled as to the first through fifth causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action. The first five causes of action are adequately pled. Whether the contractual term "fixture" covers some of all of the sewer system is not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. A claim for breach of the warranty of habitability is not available unless there are inhabitable conditions, but that is not the case for a non-residential restaurant. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ALICE TOY AND HA HUYNH'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Stanley Shen (994.5853) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: off calendar as moot as to the portions of the sixth cause of action sought to be stricken and denies as to all other portions of the complaint sought to be stricken. The request for punitive damages is adequately pled. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported
